learning_outcomes.description: identify and ethically evaluate claims about economic justice
develop independent views about economic justice
be able to differentiate incentives, economic rents, and compensation and to use these concepts in developing solutions to problems of economic justice
characterise some of the practical policy problems in achieving more social and economic justice
identify some of the causes of income inequality and use that knowledge to analyse some economic justice policies
communicate effectively in writing about economic justice

assessments.task_description:
Participation in the lectures and tutorials includes in-class quizzes each week. The participation assessment is there to encourage students to engage in active analytical and critical thinking and to prepare by reading the required reading each week. Its purposes include helping students learn how to think philosophically (philosophy is not, like many disciplines, mainly about learning content but rather more about developing reasoning skills), staying current with the material being discussed and, consequently, getting more long-term benefit from the class.

Allowance is built into the design of the participation mark for students, without penalty, not to participate in up to one lecture or two tutorials for reasons not recognized by the university as acceptable exemptions from assessment (e.g. medical grounds or where there are exceptional circumstances - http://www.uq.edu.au/myadvisor/index.html?page=2995 ). Please do not contact the lecturer if you are absent from a class unless you will be applying for one of the University acceptable exemptions to assessment.

The "Lecture and Tutorial Participation Assessment Criteria" described at the bottom of the Assessment section provides the criteria.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.due_date:
Week 13, Mon 4:00 pm

The In-Class Test will be held during the lecture on Monday 26 October. The test requires you to attend with a fully charged laptop with the requisite Inspera software installed - this is entirely the student's responsibility. The test will have strict word limits and an extended time limit for inclusion of students on SAPs.
assessments.task_description:
50 minute in-class Test

 The "Exam and Test Criteria" described at the bottom of the Assessment section provides the criteria.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The exam will be submitted via the exam software.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
Week 11 - Week 13

Your powerpoint slides for your presentation are to be submitted as a powerpoint file by 2pm the day before your presentation.
assessments.task_description:
Oral Presentation on Chosen Case Study

There is an option to do the presentation with other students in the course. This is only an option - adopting this option is done solely via private voluntary arrangements between students. Group work is not a required part of the course and is not taught in this course. This option though gives students the opportunity to work collaboratively on their presentation if they wish to in a way that more closely resembles work situations. Students who choose to do their presentation jointly will receive an identical mark and feedback for the jointly submitted presentation. How the work is divided among the group (including presenting) is entirely up to the students themselves.


Time Limits (strict)

Individual: 5 minutes

Group of two: 6 minutes

Group of three: 7 minutes

There will be brief questions and answers after each presentation.


Your powerpoint slides for your presentation are to be submitted as a powerpoint file by 2pm the day before your presentation. The word count of the presentation powerpoint should not exceed 500 words for single presentation, 600 words for double presentation, 700 words for triple presentation. Diagrams, etc count 40 words.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.

Introduction and Development of Economic Justice

Introduction; Conceptualization and Development of Economic Justice


Learning outcomes: L01
learning_activities.topic_description: Introduction and Development of Economic Justice Introduction; Conceptualization and Development of Economic Justice

learning_activities.topic:
Schedule and Tutorial Information

The Learning Activities Schedule will be revised as the semester proceeds with additional material as needed - please check the schedule each week for updates.

Students are to attend one 2 hour lecture and a 1 hour tutorial each week. The tutorials start in Week 2. There will be 10 tutorials for each tutorial class during the semester. The tutorials occur after the lecture and will involve continuation of the discussion of the lecture topic for that week, plus discussion of the readings for that week.
